cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what made Woolworth a world-famous man. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to recall the details from the passage. The passage starts by talking about Woolworth's humble beginnings. He decided not to be a farmer, took a business course, worked as a salesman. Then he realized he had a skill for displaying goods to attract interest. The key moment was when he sold odds and ends at five cents each, and people rushed to buy. That led him to open his own store with five and ten cent items. But there's another point mentioned: he learned that to make money with low-price goods, he needed to buy in large quantities directly from factories. The example given was his order of knives from Germany, which required the factory to run 24/7 for a year, cutting the price in half. This strategy helped him reduce costs and offer lower prices. By 1919, he had 1000 stores and expanded to London. He made many millions and became famous worldwide. His business was based on strict rules, the most important being "The customer is always right." Now, the question is about what made him world-famous. The options are: A. His business skills and his wealth B. The low price of the goods he sold C. His trip to Germany and his huge order of knives D. His natural skill for displaying things Looking at the
